title: Brachytherapy Dose Imaging
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical medical-imaging literature for this principle.

E.description: Brachytherapy Dose Imaging: brachytherapy dose produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG L.sealed_gamma_source -> L.inverse_square_decay -> int.spatial, with spatially-projected accumulation and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 3D dose distribution. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~10; calibration-level mismatch (source_position_error, tissue_heterogeneity, inter_source_shielding) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: D(r) = sum_i A_i / |r - r_i|^2 * tissue_correction + n; TG-43 formalism
E.world_state_x: Patient-side quantity of interest (attenuation / activity / perfusion / stiffness etc.)
E.observation_y: Clinical sensor output at declared resolution + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D dose distribution is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 10); source_position_error dominates the stability cliff; tissue_heterogeneity and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: analytic / closed-form + post-processing [TG-43] | linear-operator + convex optimisation [Model-Based] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [Brachy-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |source_position_error| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
